metadata.topic: Cost-Quality Pareto Optimization for Multi-Agent Topology Selection

papers.title: FrugalGPT: How to Use Large Language Models While Reducing Cost and Improving Performance
papers.contribution: Three strategies for cost reduction: prompt adaptation, LLM approximation, and LLM cascade. Matches GPT-4 performance with up to 98% cost reduction.
papers.limitation: Cascading strategy requires labeled calibration data; does not consider multi-agent topologies beyond sequential escalation.

papers.title: RouteLLM: Learning to Route LLMs with Preference Data
papers.contribution: Uses human preference data to train routers selecting between strong/weak LLMs, achieving 85%+ cost reduction on MT-Bench.
papers.limitation: Binary strong/weak routing only; does not model multi-agent orchestration topologies or task decomposition features.

papers.title: Hybrid LLM: Cost-Efficient and Quality-Aware Query Routing
papers.contribution: Difficulty-aware router assigning queries to small or large models; up to 40% fewer calls to the large model with no quality drop.
papers.limitation: Two-model routing only; does not consider orchestration topology as a dimension of the routing decision.

papers.title: EcoAssistant: Using LLM Assistant More Affordably and Accurately
papers.contribution: Hierarchical LLM cascade with solution retrieval; surpasses GPT-4 by 10% success rate at less than 50% cost.
papers.limitation: Cascade is sequential (small to large); does not explore parallel multi-agent topologies.

papers.title: AutoMix: Automatically Mixing Language Models
papers.contribution: POMDP-based router with few-shot self-verification routing queries from small to large models; reduces cost by 50%+.
papers.limitation: Self-verification adds latency; single-query routing without task-level feature extraction.

papers.title: OptLLM: Optimal Assignment of Queries to Large Language Models
papers.contribution: Multi-objective optimization with Pareto-dominant solutions for query-LLM assignment; saves 2.4%-49.2% cost at same accuracy.
papers.limitation: Static assignment; does not adapt online or consider orchestration topologies.

papers.title: RouterBench: A Benchmark for Multi-LLM Routing System
papers.contribution: 405K+ inference outcomes from 11 models across 64 tasks; provides theoretical framework for evaluating routing strategies.
papers.limitation: Evaluates model routing but not topology routing; all runs are single-agent.

papers.title: A Unified Approach to Routing and Cascading for LLMs
papers.contribution: Proves cascade routing is theoretically optimal; unifies routing and cascading into a single framework.
papers.limitation: Theoretical framework for model cascading; does not address multi-agent coordination topologies.

papers.title: Efficient Contextual LLM Cascades through Budget-Constrained Policy Learning
papers.contribution: RL-based policy jointly selecting model and prompting scheme under monetary cost and latency constraints.
papers.limitation: Budget constraints are per-query; does not model topology-level cost structures.

papers.title: Cascade-Aware Training of Language Models
papers.contribution: Trains small LMs aware of cascade position using large model predictions; improved cost-quality tradeoffs.
papers.limitation: Requires access to large model outputs during training; cascade-specific, not topology-general.

papers.title: Pareto Optimal Learning for Estimating Large Language Model Errors
papers.contribution: Pareto-optimal error estimation integrating multiple information sources to predict LLM response reliability.
papers.limitation: Error estimation only; does not directly optimize for cost-quality routing.

papers.title: Language Model Cascades
papers.contribution: Formalizes LM compositions as probabilistic programs; unifies scratchpads, chain-of-thought, verifiers, and tool use.
papers.limitation: Theoretical formalization; no empirical cost analysis of different cascade configurations.

papers.title: Large Language Models as Tool Makers
papers.contribution: GPT-4 creates reusable tools; GPT-3.5 uses them. Amortizes tool-making cost over many uses.
papers.limitation: Two-tier model only; tool creation cost not always amortizable for one-off tasks.

papers.title: Fast Inference from Transformers via Speculative Decoding
papers.contribution: Draft model generates candidates verified in parallel by target model; 2-3x speedup with identical output distribution.
papers.limitation: Token-level optimization; does not address task-level cost-quality routing.

papers.title: Accelerating Large Language Model Decoding with Speculative Sampling
papers.contribution: Modified rejection sampling preserving target distribution; 2-2.5x speedup on Chinchilla 70B.
papers.limitation: Latency optimization only; does not reduce total compute or API cost.

papers.title: Outrageously Large Neural Networks: The Sparsely-Gated Mixture-of-Experts Layer
papers.contribution: Sparsely-gated MoE enabling 1000x model capacity with minor compute overhead via conditional computation.
papers.limitation: Model-internal routing; does not address multi-agent orchestration.

papers.title: MetaGPT: Meta Programming for A Multi-Agent Collaborative Framework
papers.contribution: Encodes SOPs into multi-agent workflows with role specialization; structured artifacts reduce error propagation.
papers.limitation: Fixed topology; no cost-aware topology selection.

papers.title: ChatDev: Communicative Agents for Software Development
papers.contribution: Chat-powered software development completing projects in <7 minutes at <$1.
papers.limitation: Waterfall-only topology; does not compare against alternative agent architectures.

papers.title: AutoGen: Enabling Next-Gen LLM Applications via Multi-Agent Conversation
papers.contribution: Open-source framework for customizable, conversable agents with combinations of LLMs, humans, and tools.
papers.limitation: Framework provides flexibility but no guidance on topology selection or cost optimization.

papers.title: Voyager: An Open-Ended Embodied Agent with Large Language Models
papers.contribution: First LLM-powered lifelong learning agent with automatic curriculum and skill library; 3.3x more unique items than prior SOTA.
papers.limitation: Single-agent with tool library; does not compare multi-agent topologies.

papers.title: Improving Factuality and Reasoning in Language Models through Multiagent Debate
papers.contribution: Multiple LLM instances debate to improve factuality and reasoning; works on black-box models.
papers.limitation: Multi-round debate is expensive; no cost analysis or comparison against cheaper alternatives.

papers.title: Difficulty-Aware Agent Orchestration in LLM-Powered Workflows
papers.contribution: VAE-based difficulty estimation + modular operator allocator + cost-aware LLM router; achieves SOTA on 6 benchmarks at 64% inference cost.
papers.limitation: Published concurrently; focuses on operator-level not topology-level cost optimization.

papers.title: Chain-of-Thought Prompting Elicits Reasoning in Large Language Models
papers.contribution: Intermediate reasoning steps via prompting dramatically improve arithmetic, commonsense, and symbolic reasoning.
papers.limitation: Increases token count and cost; no analysis of when simpler prompting suffices.

papers.title: Tree of Thoughts: Deliberate Problem Solving with Large Language Models
papers.contribution: Tree search over reasoning paths with self-evaluation and backtracking; improves Game of 24 from 4% to 74%.
papers.limitation: 3-5x token cost over CoT; high cost for marginal quality gains on easier tasks.

papers.title: ReAct: Synergizing Reasoning and Acting in Language Models
papers.contribution: Interleaves reasoning traces and tool-use actions; enables grounded reasoning in external information.
papers.limitation: Token-expensive due to verbose reasoning traces; no cost-aware reasoning depth control.

papers.title: Toolformer: Language Models Can Teach Themselves to Use Tools
papers.contribution: Self-supervised tool use training; achieves competitive performance with much larger models via tool augmentation.
papers.limitation: Requires fine-tuning; tool call overhead not modeled in cost analysis.

papers.title: Gorilla: Large Language Model Connected with Massive APIs
papers.contribution: Fine-tuned LLaMA surpasses GPT-4 on API call generation; adapts to document changes at test time.
papers.limitation: API call accuracy only; does not consider full task completion or cost implications.

papers.title: GAIA: A Benchmark for General AI Assistants
papers.contribution: 466 real-world questions requiring reasoning, web browsing, and tool use; humans 92% vs GPT-4 15%.
papers.limitation: Difficulty levels defined but DIT-style task characterization not provided.

papers.title: SWE-bench: Can Language Models Resolve Real-World GitHub Issues?
papers.contribution: 2,294 software engineering problems from real GitHub issues; execution-based evaluation.
papers.limitation: Code-only; does not span the full range of agent task types.

papers.title: Evaluating Large Language Models Trained on Code
papers.contribution: 164 Python problems with pass@k metric; Codex 28.8%, repeated sampling 70.2%.
papers.limitation: Small benchmark; sampling-based evaluation has cost implications not analyzed.

papers.title: AgentBench: Evaluating LLMs as Agents
papers.contribution: 8 environments evaluating LLM-as-Agent; benchmarks 29 LLMs showing large commercial vs open gap.
papers.limitation: Single-agent evaluation only; does not compare multi-agent topologies.

papers.title: Finite-time Analysis of the Multiarmed Bandit Problem
papers.contribution: UCB1 achieving optimal logarithmic regret uniformly over time for bounded reward distributions.
papers.limitation: Non-contextual; does not use task features for arm selection.

papers.title: On the Likelihood that One Unknown Probability Exceeds Another in View of the Evidence of Two Samples
papers.contribution: Introduces Bayesian approach to sequential sampling (Thompson Sampling).
papers.limitation: Original formulation; modern contextual extensions needed for LLM routing.

papers.title: Thompson Sampling for Contextual Bandits with Linear Payoffs
papers.contribution: First theoretical guarantees for contextual Thompson Sampling with near-optimal regret bounds.
papers.limitation: Linear payoff assumption may not hold for non-linear cost-quality relationships.

papers.title: A Fast and Elitist Multiobjective Genetic Algorithm: NSGA-II
papers.contribution: Fast non-dominated sorting with crowding distance; de facto standard for multi-objective optimization.
papers.limitation: Evolutionary approach; may be overkill for the discrete topology selection problem.

papers.title: Pareto-Based Multiobjective Machine Learning: An Overview and Case Studies
papers.venue: IEEE Transactions on Systems, Man, and Cybernetics, Part C
papers.contribution: Shows ML is inherently multi-objective; Pareto approaches outperform scalar cost functions.
papers.limitation: Pre-LLM era; principles apply but specific methods need adaptation.

papers.title: Pareto Optimal Model Selection in Linear Bandits
papers.contribution: Pareto-optimal regret guarantees for model selection in linear bandits.
papers.limitation: Linear bandit setting; extension to contextual non-linear settings non-trivial.

papers.title: The Architecture of Complexity
papers.venue: Proceedings of the American Philosophical Society, 106:467-482
papers.contribution: Near-decomposability theory; complex systems evolve faster with stable hierarchical forms.
papers.limitation: Theoretical; requires operationalization for computational task decomposition.

papers.title: Designing for Complexity: Using Divisions and Hierarchy to Manage Complex Tasks
papers.contribution: Divisionalization depends on task interdependence and decomposability; hierarchy mitigates complexity.
papers.limitation: Organizational science; metaphorical application to LLM agent teams.

papers.title: C3PO: Optimized Large Language Model Cascades with Probabilistic Cost Constraints for Reasoning
papers.contribution: Self-supervised cascade optimization with conformal prediction for probabilistic cost bounds.
papers.limitation: Reasoning-focused cascading; does not address multi-agent topology diversity.

papers.title: Model Cascading for Code: Reducing Inference Costs with Model Cascading for LLM Based Code Generation
papers.contribution: Cascading + self-testing for code generation; reduces costs by average 26%, up to 70%.
papers.limitation: Domain-specific (code); self-testing accuracy varies by task difficulty.

papers.title: Compute-Accuracy Pareto Frontiers for Open-Source Reasoning Large Language Models
papers.contribution: Test-time-compute aware Pareto frontiers across math and reasoning benchmarks for open-source LLMs.
papers.limitation: Test-time compute focus; does not consider multi-agent orchestration costs.

papers.title: Dynamic Model Routing and Cascading for Efficient LLM Inference: A Survey
papers.contribution: Comprehensive survey of routing and cascading approaches for efficient LLM inference.
papers.limitation: Survey; no new methods proposed.

papers.title: Multi-Agent Collaboration via Evolving Orchestration
papers.contribution: RL-trained centralized orchestrator dynamically sequences agents; reduces computational costs.
papers.limitation: Learned orchestration policy may not generalize across task domains.

papers.title: Online Cascade Learning for Efficient Inference over Streams
papers.contribution: Cascade learning as imitation learning; learns deferral policies for streaming inference.
papers.limitation: Streaming focus; batch task routing not addressed.

papers.title: LLM Cascade with Multi-Objective Optimal Consideration
papers.contribution: Multi-objective optimization for LLM cascades balancing accuracy, cost, and latency.
papers.limitation: Cascade-only (sequential escalation); does not consider parallel multi-agent topologies.

papers.title: The Pareto Frontier of model selection for general Contextual Bandits
papers.contribution: Characterizes Pareto frontier of achievable regret bounds for model selection in contextual bandits.
papers.limitation: Theoretical; practical implementation for LLM routing not demonstrated.

papers.title: Bayesian Orchestration of Multi-LLM Agents for Cost-Aware Sequential Decision-Making
papers.contribution: Bayesian framework for optimal sequential decision-making across multiple LLMs.
papers.limitation: Sequential decision focus; does not model parallel multi-agent topologies.

papers.title: Multi-Objective Deep Learning: Taxonomy and Survey of the State of the Art
papers.contribution: Taxonomy of multi-objective deep learning methods covering training algorithms.
papers.limitation: Broad survey; LLM routing is a small part of the covered landscape.

papers.title: Some Aspects of the Sequential Design of Experiments
papers.venue: Bulletin of the American Mathematical Society, 58(5):527-535
papers.contribution: Formalizes the multi-armed bandit problem and introduces regret as a performance measure.
papers.limitation: Foundational; modern extensions needed for contextual settings.
